lo.rules.parse_structure.label: Parse structure
lo.rules.parse_structure.body:
Splits the raw source text into ordered topic sections by Markdown headings, dropping recap/summary and empty sections, and emits a structured section list for the rest of the pipeline (deterministic — no LLM prompt; this documents the hard-coded rules).

HEADING SPLITTING
- A line counts as a topic heading only when it matches the regex ^(#{1,3})\s+(.*\S)\s*$ (1 to 3 leading # characters, whitespace, then non-empty title text).
- A new section is started only for level-1 and level-2 headings: the match is accepted only when the number of leading # characters is <= 2 (# and ## split; ### never starts a new section and is treated as body text).
- The heading title is the captured text after the # marks, stripped of surrounding whitespace.

FENCED CODE BLOCKS
- A line matching ^\s*``` (a fenced code block delimiter, allowing leading whitespace) toggles an in-fence state.
- While inside a fenced code block, heading detection is suppressed so # lines inside code are never treated as headings.

PREAMBLE AND FALLBACK
- Any non-blank text appearing before the first heading is collected into a synthetic section titled "Introduction"; the Introduction section is created by the first non-blank preamble line (purely blank lines before it are skipped), after which all following lines until the first heading are appended to it.
- If no blocks are produced at all (no headings and no non-blank preamble), the entire document is used as a single section titled "Introduction" containing all lines (text split on newlines).

RECAP AND EMPTY DROPPING
- A section title is flagged as recap when it matches the regex \b(summary|recap|revision|conclusion|cheat\s*sheet|takeaway), case-insensitive (re.I), searched anywhere in the title.
- A section is dropped if it is flagged as recap, or if its body text (the lines joined with newlines and then stripped) is empty.
- Each dropped section is recorded in the log with node "parse_structure", the dropped title under key "dropped", and reason "recap/summary" (if recap; this reason takes precedence) or "empty" (if not recap but body is empty).

EMITTED SECTION FIELDS
- topic_id: formatted as "T{order+1}_<slug>" where order is the zero-based kept-section index and <slug> is slugify(title); the whole id string is then truncated to the first 48 characters via [:48].
- title: the heading title.
- order: zero-based sequence number of the kept section, incremented only for sections that survive dropping.
- text: the section body (lines joined with newlines and stripped).
- has_code: true when the body contains the substring ``` (a fenced code block), otherwise false.

ESCALATION
- If, after dropping, no sections remain, the stage raises RuntimeError "ESCALATE: structure could not be recovered from source."

OUTPUT
- Returns sections (the list of emitted section objects) and log (the list of drop records).

lo.rules.canonicalize_concepts.label: Canonicalize concepts
lo.rules.canonicalize_concepts.body:
Collapses every raw extracted concept into a stable, deduplicated concept inventory by canonical-naming each concept, assigning it a deterministic concept_id, grounding its evidence quote to the source section, flagging procedural concepts, merging duplicates, and flagging near-duplicates (deterministic — no LLM prompt; this documents the hard-coded rules).

CANONICAL NAMING
- The raw concept name is normalized by trimming, lowercasing, and collapsing all internal whitespace runs to a single space.
- The normalized name is then looked up in ALIAS_MAP; if a key matches, its mapped canonical value is used, otherwise the normalized name is kept as-is.
- ALIAS_MAP is currently an empty mapping ({}), so no alias substitutions occur unless entries are added.
- The stored canonical_name is the canonical string converted to Title Case.

CONCEPT ID
- The concept_id is the literal prefix "C_" followed by the slug of the canonical name, i.e. "C_<slug>".
- slugify lowercases the name, replaces every run of characters that are not a-z or 0-9 with a single underscore "_", strips leading/trailing underscores, collapses repeated underscores, and falls back to "x" if the result is empty.

EVIDENCE GROUNDING
- The evidence quote is produced by ground_quote(raw concept name, section text), which extracts a VERBATIM, whitespace-collapsed snippet from the source section so the quote provably resolves to the source.
- ground_quote scans the concept name for word tokens matching [A-Za-z_]{3,}, tries them longest-first, and finds the first one present (case-insensitive) in the section text.
- On a match it returns the text from the start of that token's line, up to width=180 characters, with internal whitespace collapsed to single spaces and trimmed.
- If no token matches, it falls back to the first 180 characters of the section text, whitespace-collapsed and trimmed.
- The concept's topic_id is set to the raw concept's evidence section, and in_scope is set to True.

PROCEDURAL FLAG
- procedural is determined by is_procedural(quote, canon, section_text) and is True if EITHER the section text contains a fenced code block (the literal triple-backtick marker) OR a strong operational cue regex matches the quote, the canonical name, or the section text.
- The strong-cue regex (case-insensitive) matches the whole words: syntax, command, compute, calculate, execute, implement, invoke, debug, trace, evaluate, construct, instantiate, initialise/initialize; or the phrase "run the"; or "step" optionally followed by whitespace then a digit.
- Theory prose with no code block and no strong cue is NOT procedural.

DUPLICATE MERGE
- Concepts are accumulated into a dict keyed by concept_id, so two raw concepts that canonicalize to the same id are merged into one inventory entry.
- When merging, the kept entry's procedural flag is OR-combined: the result is procedural if either the existing entry or the new duplicate is procedural.
- On merge, the first-seen entry's canonical_name, topic_id, in_scope, and evidence are retained (only procedural is updated).

NEAR-DUPLICATE FLAGGING
- After building the inventory, every unordered pair of distinct concept_ids is compared by token-set Jaccard similarity.
- The token set for an id is the set of underscore-separated slug tokens after the "C_" prefix (id.split("_")[1:]).
- If both token sets are non-empty and |intersection| / |union| >= 0.6, a log entry is appended: {"node": "canonicalize", "flag": "near_duplicate", "pair": [a, b]}. This is a flag only; the concepts are not merged.

SORT ORDER
- The final concept_inventory is emitted with entries ordered by ascending concept_id (sorted lexicographically).
- The stage returns concept_inventory plus the near_duplicate log entries, and reports the total concept count and how many are procedural.

lo.rules.plan_allocation.label: Plan allocation
lo.rules.plan_allocation.body:
Distributes the fixed question budget across topics by concept weight, computes per-topic Apply suitability and capacity from the run's own concept DAG, and lowers the Apply target when capacity is short (deterministic — no LLM prompt; this documents the hard-coded rules).

SLOT ALLOCATION
- The total question budget is QUESTION_BUDGET = 20 slots, distributed across all topics (the frozen sections list, state["sections"]).
- Each topic's weight is its concept count: the number of concepts in concept_inventory whose topic_id matches the topic, floored at a minimum of 1 (max(1, count)).
- Slots are assigned by the largest-remainder method: each topic first gets the integer floor of (weight / sum-of-weights * 20); the leftover slots are then handed out one each to the topics with the largest fractional remainders, breaking ties by ascending list index.
- The weights list is built in topic order; the returned slot list is indexed back onto each topic by the topic's "order" field, which equals its position in the sections list.
- Edge case: if the sum of weights is <= 0 or the budget is <= 0, every topic gets 0 slots.
- If a topic receives 0 slots (more topics than budget), a log entry is recorded ("more topics than budget"); that topic is still emitted in the plan with 0 slots (the plan row is built first, then the log entry appended).

APPLY-SUITABILITY (PER CONCEPT)
- A concept is apply_suitable only if it is procedural (concept-level "procedural" flag is true). Non-procedural concepts are never apply_suitable.
- A procedural concept is apply_suitable when EITHER at least one of its DAG prerequisites is in scope, OR its topic's section text contains a fenced code block (the literal "```").
- Prerequisites are the full ancestor closure of the concept in the run's frozen concept DAG, via graph_find_prerequisites (inverts the run's own _adj map into parent edges, then walks the parent closure). "In scope" means a prerequisite concept_id is present among concepts whose in_scope flag is true.
- "Section has code" is computed once per topic as whether "```" appears in that topic's text.
- The computed apply_suitable flag is written back onto each concept in the returned concept_inventory.

PER-TOPIC APPLY CAPACITY
- For each topic, proc_count = the number of its concepts that are apply_suitable.
- For each topic, cap_apply = min(its allocated slots, proc_count) — a topic can never hold more Apply questions than it has slots or apply-suitable concepts.
- apply_capacity = the sum of cap_apply across all topics (also returned as apply_suitable_total).

BLOOM SPLIT AND FEASIBILITY OVERRIDE
- The default Bloom split is DEFAULT_SPLIT = 12 remember_understand / 8 apply (Apply is treated as a ceiling, not a quota).
- If apply_capacity >= 8 (the default Apply target), OR if ALLOW_SPLIT_OVERRIDE is false, the default 12/8 split is used unchanged.
- If apply_capacity < 8 and ALLOW_SPLIT_OVERRIDE is true (it is true by default), the split is overridden to apply = apply_capacity and remember_understand = 20 - apply_capacity, so the totals still sum to 20.
- An override record is appended (rule "bloom_split", from "12/8", to the new split, reason "only N apply-suitable slot(s) available (< 8)") and the same override is written to the log.

PLACING APPLY ON TOPICS
- The number of Apply questions actually placed is min(split apply, apply_capacity).
- Apply questions are placed onto the most-suitable topics first: topics are ranked by descending proc_count, ties broken by ascending topic_id.
- Apply slots are dealt round-robin in that ranked order, one per topic per pass, never exceeding any topic's cap_apply, repeating until all Apply slots are placed or no further progress can be made.
- Each topic's final per-topic Bloom counts are then: apply = the Apply slots it received, remember_understand = its total slots minus its Apply slots.

OUTPUT
- Returns concept_inventory (with apply_suitable added), an allocation_plan {by_topic, effective_split, apply_suitable_total}, the overrides list, and the log.
- Per-topic plan rows carry topic_id, title, slots, and bloom {remember_understand, apply}.
- Concept ids referenced throughout follow the form "C_<slug>" (concept_id = "C_" + slugify(name)).
- All budget, split, and override-policy values mirror the constants in lo_config.py: QUESTION_BUDGET (20), DEFAULT_SPLIT (12/8), ALLOW_SPLIT_OVERRIDE (true).

lo.rules.resolve_prerequisites.label: Resolve prerequisites
lo.rules.resolve_prerequisites.body:
Assigns each learning outcome its prerequisite concept set and a scope verdict by walking the run's frozen concept DAG (deterministic — no LLM prompt; this documents the hard-coded rules).

INPUTS
- assumed_ids = the set of "C_" + slugify(p) ids, where p ranges over the names in concept_graph.assumed_prior.
- in_scope_ids = the set of concept_id values from concept_inventory entries where in_scope is truthy.
- Operates on a shallow copy (dict(o)) of each outcome in state.outcomes; returns {"outcomes": <updated list>}.

OUTCOME GATE BY BLOOM LEVEL
- Only outcomes with bloom_level == "apply" receive a computed prerequisite closure.
- Any outcome whose bloom_level != "apply" (the remember_understand outcomes) gets prerequisites = [] (empty list) and prerequisite_scope = None.

PREREQUISITE CLOSURE (apply outcomes)
- prerequisites are the ancestor (prerequisite) closure of the outcome's concept_id in the concept DAG, computed by graph_find_prerequisites.
- The closure is computed by reading concept_graph._adj (via .get("_adj", {}), defaulting to empty), inverting the adjacency map into parent edges, then transitively collecting all ancestors via a stack-based walk; the result is returned as sorted(closure).
- The closure excludes the outcome's own concept_id (the walk starts from the concept's parents and only adds ancestors).

EMPTY-CLOSURE FALLBACK
- If the computed closure is empty (no prerequisites found in the DAG), prerequisites falls back to sorted(assumed_ids) — the sorted assumed_prior id set.

PREREQUISITE SCOPE (apply outcomes)
- prerequisite_scope = "all_in_scope" if every prerequisite id is in in_scope_ids OR in assumed_ids (ok = all(p in in_scope_ids or p in assumed_ids for p in prereqs)).
- prerequisite_scope = "has_out_of_scope" if any prerequisite id is neither in in_scope_ids nor in assumed_ids. UPDATED: this is now RAG-VERIFIED (resolve_prerequisites binds RAG) — a prerequisite must be actually TAUGHT across the accessible scope (the CURRENT session OR a prior course, via check_concept) OR be a declared assumed foundation; a non-assumed prerequisite that RAG finds NOT covered yields has_out_of_scope (V6 fails -> repair). Per-outcome prerequisite_coverage {covered, uncovered} evidence is attached; falls back to id-membership if RAG is unavailable.

lo.rules.validate.label: Validate (V1–V13)
lo.rules.validate.body:
The Validate stage runs twelve hard-coded assertions (V1–V12) plus one strict coverage-rubric gate (V13) over the authored learning outcome set (state["outcomes"]); each rule records its pass flag, a human-readable detail, and the list of failing item ids, then the router sends the run back to Repair, except once the retry cap is reached, when the run is finalized as escalated (deterministic — no LLM prompt; this documents the hard-coded rules).

BUDGET & ALLOCATION

- V1 — Budget count: asserts len(outcomes) == QUESTION_BUDGET = 20 (detail reports count vs the wanted 20).

- V2 — Bloom split: asserts the realized split matches the plan's effective_split exactly; counts how many outcomes have bloom_level == "apply" and treats the remainder as "remember_understand" (the default split DEFAULT_SPLIT is 12 remember_understand / 8 apply, with apply as a ceiling that may be lowered on theory-heavy sessions when ALLOW_SPLIT_OVERRIDE is set).

- V3 — Per-topic slots: asserts that, for every topic id in allocation_plan.by_topic, the number of outcomes whose topic_id matches equals that topic's allocated "slots"; any mismatched topic is reported as {topic, got, want}.

COVERAGE

- V4 — Concept coverage: asserts every in-scope concept (concept_id of each concept_inventory entry with in_scope == true) is covered by at least one outcome's concept_id; uncovered in-scope concept ids are listed (sorted) as failures.

PREREQUISITES (apply outcomes)

- V5 — Non-empty prerequisites: asserts no apply outcome has an empty prerequisites set; apply outcomes with no prerequisites are listed by outcome id.

- V6 — Prerequisites in scope: asserts no apply outcome has prerequisite_scope == "has_out_of_scope" (i.e. its prerequisite closure must be fully in-scope or assumed-prior); offending apply outcomes are listed by id.

- V7 — DAG acyclicity: asserts state.concept_graph.acyclic is true (the frozen concept DAG has no cycles).

APPLY INTEGRITY

- V8 — Fake-apply guard: asserts every apply outcome (a) has a learner_action in the APPLY_VERBS vocabulary {execute, implement, apply, write, compute, solve, construct, use, modify, demonstrate, calculate, debug, trace, develop, build, perform, produce} AND (b) targets a procedural concept — the per-concept signal is c.get("apply_suitable", c["procedural"]) (apply_suitable, falling back to procedural) and must be truthy; any apply outcome with a non-apply verb or a non-procedural concept is listed by id.

GROUNDING

- V9 — Evidence grounding: asserts each outcome's source_evidence.quote is non-empty (after strip) and that the first 60 characters of its loosened (markdown/whitespace-flattened via loosen_text: lowercased, [*_`#>]+ stripped, whitespace collapsed) form appear inside the loosened source_text; outcomes whose evidence cannot be found in the source are listed by id.

- V11 — Apply syntax grounding: asserts every apply outcome that carries a truthy syntax passes syntax_grounded(o["syntax"], source_text) — syntax is grounded if it is null/empty, OR its loosened form is a substring of the loosened source, OR every code-like token (matching [a-z_][a-z0-9_]{2,}) it contains appears in the loosened source. Ungrounded apply syntax is listed by id.

VOCABULARY

- V10 — Controlled verb vocabulary: asserts each outcome's learner_action belongs to VERBS[bloom_level] — VERBS["remember_understand"] = RU_VERBS = {define, identify, describe, explain, summarize, classify, distinguish, compare, illustrate, recognize, label, list, match, interpret, outline, differentiate} and VERBS["apply"] = APPLY_VERBS (above); outcomes whose verb is outside the allowed set for their level are listed by id.

GROUNDING DEPTH

- V12 — Over-reach guard: asserts no remember_understand outcome demands more than the material TEACHES. A comparison verb (compare/distinguish/differentiate/contrast) requires the source to explicitly contrast the outcome's entities (a contrast cue in a sentence mentioning one of them, via has_explicit_contrast); any other non-recall (elaboration) verb requires the concept to appear in more than one source sentence (concept_depth > 1; recall verbs = identify/list/label/recognize/match/name/define/state). Over-reaching outcomes are listed by id and routed to repair, which deterministically lowers them to a grounded recall (identify) of the concept.

- V13 — STRICT coverage gate (the coverage_gate agent, prompt lo.coverage_rubric; read here): scores each outcome against a 5-criterion rubric — R1 the concept is explicitly taught, R2 the depth matches the verb's demand, R3 it is answerable from the material alone, R4 it makes no beyond-scope leap (THE key failure: concept covered but the outcome reaches past what's taught), R5 the answer key is derivable. covered = all five pass. Verdicts live in state['lo_reviews'] keyed by outcome id ({covered, rubric, beyond_coverage_reason, suggested_recall_title}); validate only READS them so it stays pure. NOT-covered outcomes route to repair, which deterministically lowers them to a covered recall. Absent verdicts (gate disabled or LLM unavailable) never flag.

ROUTING ON FAILURE

- Failure routing: the validation_report records each rule as {pass, detail, failing}; route_after_validate collects all rules where pass is false. If none failed, the run proceeds to finalize. If any failed and retry_count is below MAX_RETRIES = 2, the run is routed to repair (which increments retry_count and re-enters resolve_prerequisites → validate). If failures remain once retry_count >= MAX_RETRIES (2), the run is sent to finalize anyway and flagged for escalation (status NEEDS_REVIEW with an escalation payload carrying reason, session_id, failed_rules, the failing report entries, and retry_count) rather than raising.

lo.rules.finalize.label: Finalize & freeze
lo.rules.finalize.body:
Assembles the validated learning outcomes into a single ordered, versioned, content-hashed artifact and marks it FROZEN or NEEDS_REVIEW, carried in run state and persisted by the backend on McqRun.result (deterministic — no LLM prompt; this documents the hard-coded rules).

STATUS
- Status is FROZEN when no validation rule failed; otherwise NEEDS_REVIEW.
- A rule is treated as failed when its entry in validation_report has pass != true (read via v.get("pass") being falsy).
- This stage NEVER raises. Finalize is only reached for failing runs after retries are exhausted (still-fixable runs are routed to repair beforehand); failures are flagged, not thrown (per section 16).

ESCALATION PAYLOAD (only when one or more rules failed)
- An escalation object is attached to both the artifact (artifact["escalation"]) and the stage output.
- reason: the fixed string "validation failed after max retries".
- session_id: the run's session_id.
- failed_rules: the list of validation_report keys whose pass != true.
- report: the subset of validation_report containing only the failing rule entries (same falsy-pass filter).
- retry_count: state["retry_count"], defaulting to 0 if absent.

OUTCOME ORDERING
- Outcomes are sorted by a 2-key tuple: (topic order, id).
- Topic order is looked up per outcome from the sections list (mapping topic_id -> order); an outcome whose topic_id is not found in sections sorts with order 99.
- Ties on topic order are broken by the outcome id (ascending string order).

APPLY-SYNTAX SAFETY NET
- After ordering, each outcome with bloom_level == "apply" that carries a non-empty (truthy) syntax is checked against the source.
- If that syntax does not ground in the source text (per syntax_grounded), its syntax is nulled (set to None) rather than shipped — the repair-then-null policy, section 16.
- Grounding rule (syntax_grounded): a null/empty syntax is always considered grounded; otherwise both source and syntax are flattened with loosen_text (lowercased first, then each run of the markdown chars *_`#>` replaced with a space, then all whitespace collapsed to single spaces and trimmed) and the syntax is grounded if the flattened snippet is empty or appears as a substring of the flattened source, or, failing that, if every token matched by the pattern [a-z_][a-z0-9_]{2,} in the snippet is present in the flattened source (a snippet with no such tokens is grounded).

HASHES / FINGERPRINTS
- spec_hash = "sha256:" + sha256 hex digest of the canonical outcomes JSON, where canonical = json.dumps(outcomes, sort_keys=True, ensure_ascii=False) encoded as UTF-8 (computed AFTER ordering and the Apply-syntax nulling).
- source_fingerprint = {"reading": "sha256:" + sha256 hex digest of state["source_text"] encoded as UTF-8}.

ARTIFACT HEADER FIELDS
- session_id: the run's session_id.
- spec_version: SPEC_VERSION = "1.0.0".
- status: FROZEN or NEEDS_REVIEW (as above).
- frozen_at: today's date in ISO format (date.today().isoformat()) when status == FROZEN; None when NEEDS_REVIEW.
- spec_hash and source_fingerprint: as defined above.
- question_budget: QUESTION_BUDGET = 20.
- effective_bloom_split: {"remember_understand": (total outcomes minus apply count), "apply": (count of outcomes with bloom_level == "apply")} — the ACTUAL split realized by the frozen outcomes, not the configured default DEFAULT_SPLIT of 12/8.
- overrides: state["overrides"], defaulting to an empty list.
- validation_report: state["validation_report"], defaulting to an empty object.
- outcomes: the ordered, syntax-sanitized outcomes list.
- escalation: present only when one or more rules failed (see ESCALATION PAYLOAD).

OUTPUT
- Returns a partial-state update containing the artifact and the (ordered, sanitized) outcomes; the escalation object is also surfaced at top level when present.
- The artifact is carried in state only and is NOT written to disk; the backend persists it on McqRun.result.

lo.rules.lo_to_legacy.label: Bridge to questions
lo.rules.lo_to_legacy.body:
Converts each new AuthoredOutcome into the legacy LearningOutcome dict shape that the question pipeline (recommend_for_los / generate_for_los / review_and_fix_for_los) consumes, merging the in-session prerequisite closure with the DB cross-course prereq units (deterministic — no LLM prompt; this documents the hard-coded rules).

INPUTS
- build_final_los reads the frozen outcomes from state["artifact"]["outcomes"], falling back to state["outcomes"] (default []) when that is missing or empty/falsy.
- It builds inv_by_id, a lookup of the run's concept_inventory keyed by concept_id, and calls lo_to_legacy once per outcome.
- db_prereq_units is passed in by the caller (the DB cross-course prerequisite units) and is attached unchanged to every produced LearningOutcome.

IDENTITY AND CONCEPT MAPPING
- outcome -> the AuthoredOutcome's id.
- concept_id -> the AuthoredOutcome's concept_id (cid); empty string if absent. Emitted as a field on the legacy dict.
- concept -> the matched inventory entry's canonical_name; if that is falsy/missing, fall back to the outcome's title, then to cid.
- sub_concept -> the concept_id with its leading "C_" stripped (cid[2:] when cid starts with "C_"); otherwise cid unchanged. Concept ids follow the "C_<slug>" convention.

BLOOM MAPPING (LOSSY)
- bloom_level is mapped through the lossy 2->legacy table: "apply" -> "apply", "remember_understand" -> "understand"; any unknown/missing value defaults to "understand".
- Both bloom_category and bloom_level on the legacy dict are set to this mapped value.
- bloom_level_raw preserves the original (unmapped) AuthoredOutcome bloom_level for audit.

SKILL TYPE
- skill_type -> the outcome's skill_type if present (truthy); otherwise derived from the mapped bloom: "practical_application" when bloom == "apply", else "conceptual".

TEXT / EVIDENCE FIELDS
- description -> the outcome's description, falling back to title, then to "".
- learner_action -> the outcome's learner_action, or "".
- syntax -> the outcome's syntax, or "".
- justification -> the outcome's justification, or "".
- source_evidence -> the "quote" field of the outcome's source_evidence object (source_evidence treated as {} when missing), or "".

PREREQUISITES (AUDIT-ONLY, NOT READ BY GENERATION)
- prerequisites -> db_prereq_units, the DB cross-course prerequisite units passed in by the caller, carried through verbatim for audit / UI.
- in_session_prerequisites -> the concept-DAG closure names: each id in the outcome's prerequisites list resolved to that concept's canonical_name via inv_by_id, falling back to the raw prerequisite id when the canonical_name key is absent.
- Neither prerequisites nor in_session_prerequisites is consumed by question generation, which keys off concept / sub_concept / syntax / source_evidence; both are carried for audit / UI only.
- prerequisite_scope -> the outcome's prerequisite_scope, passed through unchanged.
